title: Project card and experiment plan

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Project card and experiment plan; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: An AI project card defines users and affected non-users, decision context, target or task, data authority, baseline, metrics, slices, risks, non-goals, oversight, acceptance thresholds, and stop criteria before fitting.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A ticket-routing card names intake-time prediction, reviewer workload baseline, urgent-miss tolerance, excluded decisions, data retention, appeal, and a stop rule for subgroup harm. Your goal is to produce an admin-reviewable project and experiment plan before model fitting. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Evaluation and responsible AI; Language, perception and embodied applications; Problem, target and success measure; Govern, map, measure and manage. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: An AI project card defines users and affected non-users, decision context, target or task, data authority, baseline, metrics, slices, risks, non-goals, oversight, acceptance thresholds, and stop criteria before fitting. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: A technical prototype may not improve the decision, available data may be unauthorized or unrepresentative, acceptance metrics can conflict, scope can expand silently, and project cancellation is a valid outcome. The learner must reject this overclaim: A project plan should start with the most promising model and add users, metrics, and risks after a prototype demonstrates accuracy.

includes: Document users, decision context, data, baseline, metrics, risks, non-goals and acceptance or stop criteria.
Produce an admin-reviewable project and experiment plan before model fitting.
excludes: A technical prototype may not improve the decision, available data may be unauthorized or unrepresentative, acceptance metrics can conflict, scope can expand silently, and project cancellation is a valid outcome.

outcomes: Produce an admin-reviewable project and experiment plan before model fitting.

examples.title: Project card and experiment plan: bounded direct case
examples.situation: A ticket-routing card names intake-time prediction, reviewer workload baseline, urgent-miss tolerance, excluded decisions, data retention, appeal, and a stop rule for subgroup harm.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: A ticket-routing card names intake-time prediction, reviewer workload baseline, urgent-miss tolerance, excluded decisions, data retention, appeal, and a stop rule for subgroup harm.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.

examples.title: Project card and experiment plan: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: The team declines a model when a deterministic queue rule meets the outcome with lower privacy and operational risk.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: The team declines a model when a deterministic queue rule meets the outcome with lower privacy and operational risk.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: A technical prototype may not improve the decision, available data may be unauthorized or unrepresentative, acceptance metrics can conflict, scope can expand silently, and project cancellation is a valid outcome.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: A project plan should start with the most promising model and add users, metrics, and risks after a prototype demonstrates accuracy.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Produce an admin-reviewable project and experiment plan before model fitting.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A project plan should start with the most promising model and add users, metrics, and risks after a prototype demonstrates accuracy.
misconceptions.correction: Define the decision, stakeholders, data authority, baseline, evidence plan, non-goals, risks, acceptance, and stop criteria before model fitting.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: A project plan should start with the most promising model and add users, metrics, and risks after a prototype demonstrates accuracy.

analogy.example: a reviewable experiment package containing scope, baseline, data/version manifests, evaluation, model card, risk register, and reproduction commands can introduce Project card and experiment plan only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: project design, reproducibility, oversight documentation, and capstone defense
limitations: documentation and a polished demo are bounded evidence, not production authorization or proof of safe capability

recap.summary: You should now be able to produce an admin-reviewable project and experiment plan before model fitting. Keep this boundary in view: A technical prototype may not improve the decision, available data may be unauthorized or unrepresentative, acceptance metrics can conflict, scope can expand silently, and project cancellation is a valid outcome.
